methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $250M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Kodiak Robotics is a Series C-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $250M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 same-sector peers at similar or adjacent stages, including 8 private companies (Saronic, Chaos Industries, Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Hidden Level, Anduril Industries, and Flock Safety) and 2 public references (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($250M) as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier typical for the sector, implies a valuation of $425M. Peer-median analysis across the 10-company comparable set yields a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B—the wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in stage advancement and capital intensity within Defense & Security. The peer set includes Series D leaders like Saronic ($9.2B) and Chaos Industries ($4.5B), as well as Series B-stage peers valued between $1.4B and $1.5B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $361M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $900M. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median implies reflects Kodiak's stage positioning relative to more mature peers. Key uncertainties include timing and evidence of product-market fit, capital efficiency relative to peers, and sector-specific risk factors in Defense & Security contracting.
